title: bootstrap.sh: fix relative source path and backtick ssh-agent invocation
description: Two issues in bootstrap.sh: (1) Line 180 uses source './scripts/install.sh' (relative path) instead of source "$THIS_DIR/scripts/install.sh" — breaks if called from non-repo directory. (2) Line 162 uses backtick eval `ssh-agent -s` (deprecated, fragile) instead of eval "$(ssh-agent -s)". All other sourcing in the file correctly uses $THIS_DIR.

title: bootstrap.sh: fix ssh-add -K deprecation (broken on macOS Ventura+)
description: bootstrap.sh:165-166 uses ssh-add -K which was removed in macOS Ventura and replaced with --apple-use-keychain. The current code silently succeeds (exit 0) without actually adding keys to the keychain on modern macOS. This is noted in docs/plans/2026-03-01-linux-compat-plan.md but never fixed. Fix: use --apple-use-keychain with fallback to plain ssh-add.

title: bootstrap.sh: add set -euo pipefail for error safety
description: bootstrap.sh is the most consequential script in the repo but has no error handling mode. A failed git clone, chmod, or gpgconf call silently continues execution. agent/setup-user.sh and scripts/bootstrap-test.sh both use set -euo pipefail — bootstrap.sh should too. Without it, irreversible operations (SSH key generation, symlinks, gnupg permissions) can silently continue after failures.

title: lib/notify.sh: fix AppleScript quote injection in osascript message
description: lib/notify.sh:38 uses string interpolation to build an osascript command: osascript -e 'display notification "'"$MESSAGE"'" ...'. If $MESSAGE contains a double-quote character, the AppleScript string is broken (quote injection). Fix: use osascript with heredoc input or properly escape the message via python3/jq JSON encoding before passing to osascript.

title: scripts/install.sh: remove dead code targeting RHEL 7 (EOL June 2024)
description: scripts/install.sh targets yum-based RHEL/CentOS 7 with EPEL 7 repos (epel-release-latest-7.noarch.rpm). RHEL 7 reached end-of-life in June 2024. It also installs yarn via a deprecated RPM repo and tries brew install tmux@3.1 inside a yum-detected context (line 24) which is contradictory. The file is sourced by bootstrap.sh on every run but only acts if yum is present — harmless but misleading dead code. Either remove or update to target current distros.

title: lib/commands.sh: fix unquoted variables causing word-splitting and injection risks
description: Multiple unquoted variable issues in lib/commands.sh: (1) Lines 89, 229: echo ${RESULT//\*} unquoted — word-splitting and glob expansion apply to branch names with spaces/glob chars. (2) Line 167: eval "$(docker-machine env $dm_name)" — $dm_name unquoted is a command injection vector. (3) Lines 92-93, 232-233: branch name from fzf composed into eval string via string concatenation — direct git checkout "$CLEANED_RESULT" would be safer.

title: bash_profile: fix hardcoded /Users/tribou/ path (line 222)
description: bash_profile:222 has a hardcoded absolute path: export PATH="/Users/tribou/.antigravity/antigravity/bin:$PATH". On any machine where the username is not 'tribou', this PATH entry silently points nowhere. The guard also uses the hardcoded path. Fix: use $HOME instead of /Users/tribou/.

title: bootstrap.sh: replace [ -s "$(which cmd)" ] with command -v
description: The antipattern [ -s "$(which cmd)" ] appears in 6 places in bootstrap.sh (lines 182, 185, 233, 355, 362, 370). This tests whether the binary path string is non-empty, which is coincidentally correct on most systems but semantically wrong and fails on some PATH setups. The idiomatic check is: command -v cmd &>/dev/null. The correct idiom is already used in the same file at line 262.

title: Add bun to the bootstrap.sh script install for both mac and linux
Guard init.vim require() calls with pcall to prevent errors on fresh install
description: On a fresh Linux server, bootstrap.sh runs nvim --headless PlugInstall before plugins exist. init.vim lines 190 (CopilotChat) and 332 (colorizer) have unguarded require() calls that fail with E5108 Lua errors. Both calls need pcall() wrapping to handle missing plugins gracefully.
close_reason: Fixed by wrapping CopilotChat and colorizer require() calls with pcall

title: Add shell startup performance budget test
description: bash_profile is ~17KB and sources ~11 lib files. NVM --no-use and lazy plugin loading are in place, but there is no regression guard. As lib/ grows, startup latency can silently degrade and hurt every shell invocation.
design: Likely a bats test that runs the timing N times, takes median, and compares to a constant. Budget lives in the test file or justfile for easy tuning. Consider also profiling via PS4 + BASH_XTRACEFD to help diagnose regressions when the test fails.
acceptance_criteria: Test measures 'time bash -i -c exit' (or equivalent reproducible harness), asserts it stays under a documented budget (e.g. 300ms), and runs in just test-unit. Failure message points at the budget so the fix is obvious.

title: Add 'just doctor' health-check command
description: Bootstrap configures many moving parts (SSH, GPG, tmux plugins, mise tools, symlinks, agent user, ~/.ssh/api_keys). There is no on-machine one-shot to verify everything is still correct after drift. just test is Docker-based (CI posture), not local health.
design: Reuse goss.yaml where possible by running goss against the host (not Docker). Add a new justfile recipe 'doctor' that runs goss + a small bash script checking symlink integrity and sourced env.
acceptance_criteria: just doctor exits non-zero when any of: required symlinks missing/broken; ~/.ssh/api_keys not sourced; GIT_SIGNING_KEY unset; expected mise tools missing; goss assertions fail on host. Output is human-readable with actionable remediation hints.

title: Remove the init_project script and the templates/ directory. They are no longer used. Remove all related references
Add new bdr command to use fzf to interactively list and search all 'bd list --all' and then run 'bd show' on the result
description:
Refer to other fzf command implementations for reference.

Add a bds and bdr alias for the _dotfiles_beads_show command that's added.

title: Organize AI context files for dotfiles repo
description: Run the organize-ai-context skill to review and improve CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and docs/ context files. Ensure all context is properly organized across the correct files with accurate content.
close_reason: All context files reviewed and updated: CLAUDE.md restructured to single CRITICAL Rules section (merged Additional Rules + non-interactive shell flags), AGENTS.md converted to pure redirect, all docs/ files enriched with missing content (scripts/, templates/, integration tests, agent isolation, etc.)

title: Merge PATTERNS.md into DEVELOPMENT.md
description: Merge duplicate information from PATTERNS.md into DEVELOPMENT.md and remove PATTERNS.md

title: Organize AI Context
description: Scan repo and create standard AI context files (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, docs/*)

title: Add a skill to help any repo organize its AI agent context into CLAUDE and AGENTS and docs/ files like this one

I think managing tmux inside tmux across ssh isn't worth the complexity. I'm considering switching to using separate alacritty sessions and ssh into the remote server at the start (without tmux) and then init a tmux session inside the remote server. The ultimate goal is to have the remote tmux session available for easy disconnect to preserve the session. This may be a better boring technology setup.
Add a quick vim shortcut to allow space, o to either run ComposerStart or ComposerOpen if the composer is already started
close_reason: Added ComposerToggle() function to functions.vim and <space>o buffer-local mapping for markdown files in keymaps.vim

title: Test stealth mode git-add fix
description: Verifying export.git-add: false suppresses the auto-export git add warning
close_reason: test issue for debugging export.git-add warning

title: Find a way to store both opencode and claude skills locally in this repo that can be invoked wherever my dotfiles are installed
close_reason: Implemented: skills/ dir, bootstrap.sh symlink, CLAUDE.md updates

title: Add mise self-update to bootstrap.sh
description: When re-running bootstrap.sh on a machine that already has mise installed, update the mise binary using mise self-update --yes. Fresh installs via curl already get latest, so update only runs on the already-installed path.

title: Test bootstrap.sh idempotency
description: docs/PRODUCT.md claims bootstrap is idempotent ('Running bootstrap repeatedly must not break anything'), but this is not tested. A whole class of regressions (duplicated lines in generated rc files, re-symlinking failures, re-running brew installs) could slip in unnoticed.
acceptance_criteria: A test (likely in tests/integration/ inside Docker) runs bootstrap.sh twice and asserts: exit code 0 both times; no duplicated lines in generated shell rc files; symlinks remain valid; no error output on second run.

title: Document or gitignore .agent/ directory
description: There is a .agent/ hidden directory at repo root that is not mentioned in docs/ARCHITECTURE.md and is distinct from the tracked agent/ directory. Readers don't know what it is, whether it is local scratch state, or whether it should be tracked.
acceptance_criteria: Either: (a) docs/ARCHITECTURE.md gains a one-liner explaining .agent/'s purpose, OR (b) .agent/ is added to .gitignore if it is local-only state. Decision recorded in the issue.

title: Clarify scripts/ vs lib/ distinction in ARCHITECTURE.md
description: ARCHITECTURE.md says lib is sourced and scripts are run directly, which is correct. But scripts/install.sh is invoked by bootstrap.sh, which can confuse readers into expecting it in lib/ or at repo root. A one-liner making the 'standalone, invoked by bootstrap' status explicit would help.
acceptance_criteria: docs/ARCHITECTURE.md Scripts section gains a sentence explicitly noting that some scripts (e.g. install.sh) are invoked by bootstrap.sh but remain standalone executables, not sourced libraries.

title: Document skills/ directory conventions
description: CLAUDE.md references skills/ but no doc explains authoring conventions, file layout, naming, or how skills are picked up by Claude Code vs opencode. Future-me (and other agents) have to reverse-engineer from the existing skills.
acceptance_criteria: Either a new docs/SKILLS.md or a section in docs/DEVELOPMENT.md covering: directory layout, required frontmatter, naming conventions, and Claude Code vs opencode discovery behavior. CLAUDE.md context index links to it.

title: Centralize platform (Darwin/Linux) guards in lib/platform.sh
description: Darwin checks are scattered across multiple lib files and bash_profile. If Linux dev-machine usage grows beyond Docker/CI, this scatter makes cross-platform changes error-prone.
acceptance_criteria: New lib/platform.sh exports is_macos / is_linux helpers. Existing Darwin checks migrated to call the helpers. Behavior unchanged; bats test covers both branches via mocked uname.

title: Warn when ~/.ssh/api_keys is missing during bootstrap
description: ~/.ssh/api_keys is silently skipped when absent (documented in docs/SECURITY.md). On a fresh machine or post-wipe, this means commits are unsigned and third-party integrations mysteriously fail with no signal. The silent-skip is intentional for shell startup but bootstrap.sh should be louder.
acceptance_criteria: bootstrap.sh prints a clear warning (not an error) when ~/.ssh/api_keys is absent, listing which env vars will be missing (e.g. GIT_SIGNING_KEY). just doctor (if shipped) also surfaces this.

title: docs/architecture.md: fix inaccurate bootstrap flags documentation
description: docs/architecture.md:7-8 documents -i / --install-deps flags for bootstrap.sh, but the actual bootstrap.sh has no such flags — it always runs all installs. The rudimentary flags parsing at lines 6-11 only handles -h/--help. Either correct the docs or implement the flag if still desired.

title: goss.yaml: decouple node version assertion from mise lts alias
description: goss.yaml:21 hardcodes node v24 in the stdout assertion while mise-config.toml uses node = "lts". When LTS moves to v26, the goss test will fail without touching mise-config.toml. Fix: either match v2[0-9] pattern, or explicitly pin the version in both files with a single source of truth.

title: lib/init_project.sh: fix unquoted variables in cp, find, sed calls
description: lib/init_project.sh:113, 116, 132-136 has unquoted variables: cp -Rn $template_dir/* $target_dir and sed calls with $file unquoted. Since this is an interactive function asking for project names (which can contain spaces), paths with spaces would break these calls. All path variables should be double-quoted.

title: lib/commands.sh: remove deprecated docker-machine functions (archived 2021)
description: lib/commands.sh contains dminit, new-docker, and new-docker-generic functions (lines 149-168, 466-503, 505-535) that reference docker-machine, which was officially archived in 2021. These functions will fail silently or error on any modern Docker Desktop setup. They add complexity with no runtime value and are candidates for removal.

title: bash_profile: prune dead legacy PATH entries (Cloud9, Antigravity, Torch, jenv, yarn)
description: bash_profile has several legacy dead PATH entries that accumulate tech debt: /opt/c9/local/bin (Cloud9 IDE, retired 2022), $HOME/.antigravity (old internal Google tool), $DEVPATH/torch/install (Lua/Torch, unsupported since ~2017), $HOME/.jenv (superseded by mise), $HOME/.yarn/bin (superseded by mise + corepack). Each is guarded by [ -d ... ] so they're safe no-ops, but slow down profile readability and signal stale thinking.
